concepts.label: Dog
concepts.definition: Domesticated carnivorous mammal, loyal companion animal kept as pet, descended from wolves

concepts.label: Cat
concepts.definition: Small carnivorous mammal, popular household pet, excellent hunter of rodents and birds

concepts.label: Golden Retriever
concepts.definition: Large friendly dog breed originally bred for retrieving waterfowl, golden-colored coat

concepts.label: Persian Cat
concepts.definition: Long-haired cat breed with flat face, originated in Persia, requires regular grooming

concepts.label: Hamster
concepts.definition: Small rodent commonly kept as pet, stores food in cheek pouches, active at night

concepts.label: Rabbit
concepts.definition: Small herbivorous mammal with long ears, powerful hind legs for hopping, soft fur

concepts.label: Guinea Pig
concepts.definition: Small domesticated rodent, social herbivore, makes squeaking sounds, popular children's pet

concepts.label: Budgerigar
concepts.definition: Small colorful parrot species, popular pet bird, can learn to mimic human speech

concepts.label: Goldfish
concepts.definition: Orange-colored freshwater fish, one of most popular aquarium pets, can live many years

concepts.label: Turtle
concepts.definition: Reptile with protective shell, can retract head and limbs inside shell when threatened

concepts.label: Ferret
concepts.definition: Small carnivorous mammal, playful and curious pet, sleeps 18-20 hours per day

concepts.label: Canary
concepts.definition: Small yellow songbird, popular for beautiful singing voice, native to Canary Islands

concepts.label: German Shepherd
concepts.definition: Large working dog breed, intelligent and loyal, commonly used as police and service dogs

concepts.label: Siamese Cat
concepts.definition: Short-haired cat breed with pointed coloration, blue eyes, vocal and social personality

concepts.label: Labrador
concepts.definition: Medium to large dog breed, friendly temperament, excellent swimmers, popular family pets

concepts.label: Chinchilla
concepts.definition: Small rodent with incredibly soft fur, native to Andes mountains, takes dust baths

concepts.label: Parakeet
concepts.definition: Small to medium parrot, colorful plumage, social birds that live in flocks

concepts.label: Beta Fish
concepts.definition: Colorful freshwater fish with flowing fins, territorial males, also called Siamese fighting fish

concepts.label: Gerbil
concepts.definition: Small desert rodent, active during day, requires very little water, social animals

concepts.label: Cockatiel
concepts.definition: Medium-sized parrot with distinctive crest feathers, grey body with yellow head

concepts.label: Pug
concepts.definition: Small dog breed with wrinkled face and curly tail, brachycephalic breathing issues

concepts.label: Maine Coon
concepts.definition: Large long-haired cat breed, bushy tail, tufted ears, gentle giant personality

concepts.label: Beagle
concepts.definition: Medium-sized hound dog breed, excellent scent detection, friendly and gentle

concepts.label: Lovebird
concepts.definition: Small colorful parrot species, forms strong pair bonds, requires social interaction

concepts.label: Angelfish
concepts.definition: Freshwater aquarium fish with triangular shape and flowing fins, peaceful temperament

concepts.label: Cow
concepts.definition: Large domesticated bovine mammal, raised for milk and meat production, grazes on grass

concepts.label: Pig
concepts.definition: Domesticated omnivorous mammal, intelligent animal raised for meat production, excellent sense of smell

concepts.label: Chicken
concepts.definition: Domesticated bird raised for eggs and meat, descendants of wild fowl, social pecking order

concepts.label: Sheep
concepts.definition: Domesticated ruminant mammal, raised for wool, milk and meat, live in flocks

concepts.label: Goat
concepts.definition: Domesticated ruminant mammal, hardy animals, raised for milk, meat and fiber

concepts.label: Horse
concepts.definition: Large domesticated mammal, used for riding and work, herbivore with single-toed hooves

concepts.label: Donkey
concepts.definition: Domesticated member of horse family, smaller than horses, used as working animal

concepts.label: Duck
concepts.definition: Waterfowl with webbed feet and waterproof feathers, raised for eggs and meat

concepts.label: Goose
concepts.definition: Large waterfowl, migratory birds, raised for meat and down feathers

concepts.label: Turkey
concepts.definition: Large domesticated bird, native to North America, raised primarily for meat

concepts.label: Milk
concepts.definition: Nutritious white liquid produced by female mammals to feed their young

concepts.label: Egg
concepts.definition: Oval reproductive structure laid by birds and some other animals

concepts.label: Wool
concepts.definition: Soft fiber obtained from sheep and other animals, used to make clothing

concepts.label: Rooster
concepts.definition: Male chicken, crows at dawn, protects flock, more colorful plumage than hens

concepts.label: Hen
concepts.definition: Female chicken, lays eggs, broods chicks, less colorful than roosters

concepts.label: Bull
concepts.definition: Male cattle, larger and more aggressive than cows, used for breeding

concepts.label: Lamb
concepts.definition: Young sheep, meat from young sheep, born in spring

concepts.label: Piglet
concepts.definition: Young pig, born in litters of 8-12, weaned at 6-8 weeks

concepts.label: Calf
concepts.definition: Young cow, drinks mother's milk, grows rapidly in first months

concepts.label: Foal
concepts.definition: Young horse, can stand and run shortly after birth

concepts.label: Duckling
concepts.definition: Young duck, covered in down feathers, follows mother closely

concepts.label: Gosling
concepts.definition: Young goose, imprints on first moving object seen after hatching

concepts.label: Ram
concepts.definition: Male sheep, has curved horns, leads flock, used for breeding

concepts.label: Mare
concepts.definition: Female horse, carries foal for 11 months, protective of young

concepts.label: Stallion
concepts.definition: Male horse, used for breeding, more muscular and aggressive than mares

concepts.label: Barn
concepts.definition: Large farm building used to store hay and house livestock

concepts.label: Pasture
concepts.definition: Grassland used for grazing livestock, fenced area for animals

concepts.label: Hay
concepts.definition: Dried grass used as animal feed, stored in bales

concepts.label: Farm
concepts.definition: Area of land used for growing crops and raising livestock

concepts.label: Lion
concepts.definition: Large carnivorous cat, apex predator of African savannas, lives in social groups called prides

concepts.label: Tiger
concepts.definition: Largest wild cat species, distinctive orange coat with black stripes, solitary hunter

concepts.label: Elephant
concepts.definition: Largest land mammal, distinctive trunk, lives in family herds, excellent memory

concepts.label: Giraffe
concepts.definition: Tallest mammal, extremely long neck and legs, lives in African savannas

concepts.label: Zebra
concepts.definition: African wild horse with distinctive black and white stripes, lives in herds

concepts.label: Monkey
concepts.definition: Primate mammal, intelligent and social, many species live in trees

concepts.label: Bear
concepts.definition: Large omnivorous mammal with thick fur, excellent sense of smell, hibernates in winter

concepts.label: Wolf
concepts.definition: Wild ancestor of domestic dogs, lives and hunts in packs, excellent communication

concepts.label: Fox
concepts.definition: Small omnivorous canine, bushy tail, cunning hunter, adapts to many environments

concepts.label: Deer
concepts.definition: Herbivorous mammal with antlers on males, graceful and fast runner, lives in forests

concepts.label: Wild Boar
concepts.definition: Wild ancestor of domestic pig, omnivorous, strong tusks, lives in forest

concepts.label: Crocodile
concepts.definition: Large aquatic reptile, powerful jaws, ambush predator, cold-blooded

concepts.label: Snake
concepts.definition: Elongated reptile without limbs, some venomous, swallows prey whole

concepts.label: Rhinoceros
concepts.definition: Large herbivorous mammal with horn on snout, thick skin, endangered species

concepts.label: Hippopotamus
concepts.definition: Large semi-aquatic mammal, spends days in water, dangerous despite herbivorous diet

concepts.label: Cheetah
concepts.definition: Fastest land animal, distinctive spotted coat, specialized for high-speed hunting

concepts.label: Leopard
concepts.definition: Large spotted cat, excellent climber, stores prey in trees

concepts.label: Gorilla
concepts.definition: Largest primate, gentle herbivore despite intimidating appearance, lives in family groups

concepts.label: Chimpanzee
concepts.definition: Intelligent primate, closest living relative to humans, uses tools

concepts.label: Orangutan
concepts.definition: Large arboreal ape with reddish hair, excellent climber, high intelligence

concepts.label: Panda
concepts.definition: Large bear species, distinctive black and white coloring, primarily eats bamboo

concepts.label: Kangaroo
concepts.definition: Large marsupial that hops on powerful hind legs, carries young in pouch

concepts.label: Koala
concepts.definition: Small marsupial, sleeps most of day, primarily eats eucalyptus leaves

concepts.label: Polar Bear
concepts.definition: Large Arctic bear with white fur, excellent swimmer, hunts seals

concepts.label: Grizzly Bear
concepts.definition: Large brown bear subspecies, omnivorous, excellent fisherman during salmon runs

concepts.label: Moose
concepts.definition: Largest member of deer family, males have broad antlers, lives in northern forests

concepts.label: Bison
concepts.definition: Large bovine mammal, distinctive humped shoulders, nearly extinct but recovering

concepts.label: Savanna
concepts.definition: Tropical grassland ecosystem with scattered trees, home to many African animals

concepts.label: Forest
concepts.definition: Large area covered with trees, provides habitat for many wildlife species

concepts.label: Jungle
concepts.definition: Dense tropical rainforest, high biodiversity, multiple canopy layers

concepts.label: Desert
concepts.definition: Dry habitat with little precipitation, extreme temperatures, specialized wildlife

concepts.label: Eagle
concepts.definition: Large bird of prey, excellent eyesight, powerful talons, soars on thermal currents

concepts.label: Sparrow
concepts.definition: Small songbird, lives in flocks, adapts well to urban environments

concepts.label: Robin
concepts.definition: Small songbird with red breast, often first bird to sing at dawn

concepts.label: Dove
concepts.definition: Small to medium bird, symbol of peace, gentle cooing call

concepts.label: Parrot
concepts.definition: Colorful tropical bird, curved beak, can mimic human speech

concepts.label: Penguin
concepts.definition: Flightless aquatic bird, lives in cold climates, excellent swimmer

concepts.label: Flamingo
concepts.definition: Wading bird with pink plumage, long curved neck, feeds on algae and small organisms

concepts.label: Owl
concepts.definition: Nocturnal bird of prey, excellent night vision, silent flight

concepts.label: Stork
concepts.definition: Large wading bird with long legs and neck, migrates long distances

concepts.label: Swan
concepts.definition: Large waterfowl with long curved neck, elegant appearance, mates for life

concepts.label: Crow
concepts.definition: Intelligent black bird, problem solver, lives in family groups

concepts.label: Woodpecker
concepts.definition: Bird that pecks holes in tree bark to find insects, distinctive drumming sound

concepts.label: Hummingbird
concepts.definition: Tiny bird that hovers while feeding on nectar, rapid wing beats

concepts.label: Cardinal
concepts.definition: Bright red songbird, non-migratory, distinctive crest on head

concepts.label: Blue Jay
concepts.definition: Intelligent blue bird, mimics calls of other birds, stores food for winter

concepts.label: Hawk
concepts.definition: Medium-sized bird of prey, keen eyesight, hunts small mammals and birds

concepts.label: Falcon
concepts.definition: Fast bird of prey, some species reach speeds over 200 mph in hunting dives

concepts.label: Seagull
concepts.definition: Coastal bird, opportunistic feeder, follows fishing boats and scavenges

concepts.label: Pelican
concepts.definition: Large water bird with distinctive throat pouch for catching fish

concepts.label: Ostrich
concepts.definition: Largest living bird, flightless but fast runner, lays enormous eggs

concepts.label: Peacock
concepts.definition: Male peafowl with spectacular tail display, iridescent blue and green plumage

concepts.label: Toucan
concepts.definition: Tropical bird with large colorful beak, lives in rainforest canopy

concepts.label: Magpie
concepts.definition: Intelligent black and white bird, collects shiny objects, complex social behavior

concepts.label: Kingfisher
concepts.definition: Colorful bird that dives into water to catch fish, excellent fishing skills

concepts.label: Beak
concepts.definition: Hard projecting jaws of a bird, adapted for different feeding methods

concepts.label: Wing
concepts.definition: Appendage used by birds for flight, covered with feathers

concepts.label: Feather
concepts.definition: Lightweight structure covering bird's body, enables flight and insulation

concepts.label: Talon
concepts.definition: Sharp curved claw of birds of prey, used for catching and holding prey

concepts.label: Nest
concepts.definition: Structure built by birds to hold eggs and raise young

concepts.label: Fish
concepts.definition: Aquatic vertebrate with gills, fins, and typically scales, lives in water

concepts.label: Shark
concepts.definition: Large predatory fish with cartilaginous skeleton, multiple rows of teeth

concepts.label: Whale
concepts.definition: Large marine mammal, breathes air through blowhole, largest animals on Earth

concepts.label: Dolphin
concepts.definition: Intelligent marine mammal, uses echolocation, lives in social groups called pods

concepts.label: Octopus
concepts.definition: Marine mollusk with eight arms, highly intelligent, can change color and texture

concepts.label: Jellyfish
concepts.definition: Marine invertebrate with gelatinous body, stinging tentacles, no brain or heart

concepts.label: Seahorse
concepts.definition: Small marine fish with horse-like head, males carry and give birth to young

concepts.label: Crab
concepts.definition: Marine crustacean with hard shell, walks sideways, has powerful claws

concepts.label: Lobster
concepts.definition: Large marine crustacean with claws, molts shell as it grows

concepts.label: Mussel
concepts.definition: Marine mollusk with two-part shell, filters water for food

concepts.label: Coral
concepts.definition: Marine invertebrate that builds reefs, symbiotic relationship with algae

concepts.label: Starfish
concepts.definition: Marine animal with five arms, can regenerate lost limbs

concepts.label: Tuna
concepts.definition: Large fast-swimming fish, important commercial species, warm-blooded

concepts.label: Salmon
concepts.definition: Fish that migrates from ocean to freshwater to spawn, important food source

concepts.label: Cod
concepts.definition: Cold-water fish, important commercial species, lives near ocean floor

concepts.label: Sea Turtle
concepts.definition: Large marine reptile, migrates long distances, returns to birth beach to nest

concepts.label: Seal
concepts.definition: Marine mammal with flippers, comes on land to breed and rest

concepts.label: Walrus
concepts.definition: Large Arctic marine mammal with tusks, lives in groups on ice floes

concepts.label: Orca
concepts.definition: Largest member of dolphin family, apex predator, highly intelligent

concepts.label: Manta Ray
concepts.definition: Large flat fish with wing-like fins, filter feeds on plankton

concepts.label: Clownfish
concepts.definition: Small colorful fish, lives symbiotically with sea anemones

concepts.label: Swordfish
concepts.definition: Large fish with sword-like bill, fast swimmer, hunts other fish

concepts.label: Barracuda
concepts.definition: Predatory fish with sharp teeth, ambush hunter, lives in tropical waters

concepts.label: Stingray
concepts.definition: Flat fish with venomous barb on tail, buried in sand on ocean floor

concepts.label: Pufferfish
concepts.definition: Fish that inflates body when threatened, some species are highly toxic

concepts.label: Ocean
concepts.definition: Large body of salt water covering most of Earth's surface

concepts.label: Reef
concepts.definition: Underwater structure made by coral, supports high biodiversity

concepts.label: Gill
concepts.definition: Respiratory organ of fish, extracts oxygen from water

concepts.label: Fin
concepts.definition: Appendage used by fish for swimming and steering

concepts.label: Bee
concepts.definition: Flying insect that collects nectar, produces honey, important pollinator

concepts.label: Ant
concepts.definition: Social insect living in colonies, incredible strength relative to size

concepts.label: Butterfly
concepts.definition: Insect with colorful wings, undergoes metamorphosis from caterpillar

concepts.label: Beetle
concepts.definition: Insect with hard wing covers, largest order of insects

concepts.label: Spider
concepts.definition: Eight-legged arachnid that spins webs to catch prey

concepts.label: Fly
concepts.definition: Two-winged insect, rapid reproduction cycle, important decomposer

concepts.label: Mosquito
concepts.definition: Small flying insect, females feed on blood, can transmit diseases

concepts.label: Dragonfly
concepts.definition: Large flying insect with four wings, excellent hunter, lives near water

concepts.label: Grasshopper
concepts.definition: Jumping insect with powerful hind legs, makes chirping sounds

concepts.label: Ladybug
concepts.definition: Small red beetle with black spots, beneficial predator of garden pests

concepts.label: Cricket
concepts.definition: Jumping insect that chirps by rubbing wings together, active at night

concepts.label: Wasp
concepts.definition: Flying insect with narrow waist, some species are social, can sting repeatedly

concepts.label: Termite
concepts.definition: Social insect that feeds on wood, builds large mounds, lives in colonies

concepts.label: Caterpillar
concepts.definition: Larval stage of butterfly or moth, feeding stage before metamorphosis

concepts.label: Moth
concepts.definition: Flying insect related to butterfly, usually active at night

concepts.label: Praying Mantis
concepts.definition: Predatory insect with raptorial forelegs, ambush hunter

concepts.label: Cockroach
concepts.definition: Hardy insect that can survive in harsh conditions, ancient lineage

concepts.label: Firefly
concepts.definition: Beetle that produces light through bioluminescence, active at dusk

concepts.label: Honey
concepts.definition: Sweet substance produced by bees from flower nectar

concepts.label: Pollination
concepts.definition: Process by which pollen is transferred from male to female flower parts

concepts.label: Metamorphosis
concepts.definition: Complete transformation from larva to adult in insects

concepts.label: Colony
concepts.definition: Group of social insects living together with division of labor

concepts.label: Web
concepts.definition: Structure spun by spiders from silk to catch prey

concepts.label: Hive
concepts.definition: Structure where bees live and store honey

concepts.label: Antenna
concepts.definition: Sensory organ on insect head for detecting chemicals and vibrations

concepts.label: Oak
concepts.definition: Large deciduous tree with lobed leaves, produces acorns, very long-lived

concepts.label: Pine
concepts.definition: Evergreen coniferous tree with needle-like leaves, produces pine cones

concepts.label: Birch
concepts.definition: Deciduous tree with distinctive white bark that peels in layers

concepts.label: Fir
concepts.definition: Evergreen coniferous tree, often used as Christmas trees

concepts.label: Apple Tree
concepts.definition: Fruit tree that produces apples, requires cross-pollination

concepts.label: Rose
concepts.definition: Flowering shrub with thorns, symbol of love, many cultivated varieties

concepts.label: Tulip
concepts.definition: Bulbous flowering plant, blooms in spring, native to Central Asia

concepts.label: Sunflower
concepts.definition: Large yellow flower that follows the sun, produces edible seeds

concepts.label: Grass
concepts.definition: Small herbaceous plant with narrow leaves, covers lawns and fields

concepts.label: Moss
concepts.definition: Small flowerless plant that grows in damp shady locations

concepts.label: Fern
concepts.definition: Flowerless plant that reproduces by spores, often has feathery fronds

concepts.label: Cactus
concepts.definition: Succulent plant adapted to arid conditions, stores water in tissues

concepts.label: Mushroom
concepts.definition: Fruiting body of fungus, some edible, others poisonous

concepts.label: Maple
concepts.definition: Deciduous tree known for colorful autumn leaves, source of maple syrup

concepts.label: Willow
concepts.definition: Tree with drooping branches, often grows near water

concepts.label: Cherry Tree
concepts.definition: Fruit tree known for beautiful spring blossoms and edible fruit

concepts.label: Lily
concepts.definition: Flowering plant with large showy blooms, grows from bulb

concepts.label: Daisy
concepts.definition: Small white flower with yellow center, symbol of innocence

concepts.label: Dandelion
concepts.definition: Yellow flowering plant, considered weed in lawns, seeds spread by wind

concepts.label: Violet
concepts.definition: Small purple flowering plant, some species edible

concepts.label: Ivy
concepts.definition: Climbing vine that can cover walls and trees

concepts.label: Root
concepts.definition: Part of plant that anchors it and absorbs water and nutrients

concepts.label: Trunk
concepts.definition: Main woody stem of tree, provides structure and transport

concepts.label: Leaf
concepts.definition: Flat organ where photosynthesis occurs, converts sunlight to energy

concepts.label: Flower
concepts.definition: Reproductive structure of plant, often colorful to attract pollinators

concepts.label: Fruit
concepts.definition: Seed-bearing structure of flowering plant, often eaten by animals

concepts.label: Seed
concepts.definition: Embryonic plant enclosed in protective coating

concepts.label: Branch
concepts.definition: Woody structural member of tree extending from trunk

concepts.label: Bark
concepts.definition: Protective outer layer of tree trunk

concepts.label: Photosynthesis
concepts.definition: Process by which plants convert sunlight into energy using chlorophyll

concepts.label: Chlorophyll
concepts.definition: Green pigment in plants that captures light energy for photosynthesis

concepts.label: Mammal
concepts.definition: Warm-blooded vertebrate that feeds milk to young and has hair or fur

concepts.label: Bird
concepts.definition: Warm-blooded egg-laying vertebrate with feathers and wings

concepts.label: Reptile
concepts.definition: Cold-blooded vertebrate with scales, typically lays eggs

concepts.label: Amphibian
concepts.definition: Cold-blooded vertebrate that lives both in water and on land

concepts.label: Insect
concepts.definition: Six-legged invertebrate with three body segments, largest group of animals

concepts.label: Carnivore
concepts.definition: Animal that primarily eats meat from other animals

concepts.label: Herbivore
concepts.definition: Animal that primarily eats plants and plant material

concepts.label: Omnivore
concepts.definition: Animal that eats both plants and animals

concepts.label: Predator
concepts.definition: Animal that hunts and kills other animals for food

concepts.label: Prey
concepts.definition: Animal that is hunted and killed by predators

concepts.label: Habitat
concepts.definition: Natural environment where organism lives and meets its needs

concepts.label: Ecosystem
concepts.definition: Community of living organisms interacting with their environment

concepts.label: Food Chain
concepts.definition: Series showing how energy passes from one organism to another

concepts.label: Migration
concepts.definition: Seasonal movement of animals from one region to another

concepts.label: Hibernation
concepts.definition: Dormant state during winter to conserve energy

concepts.label: Camouflage
concepts.definition: Ability to blend in with surroundings for protection
